title: cytisus infestus subsp. intermedius
intro: Cytisus infestus subsp. intermedius, also known as the intermediate broom, is a plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae), characterized by its dense, thorny shrub-like growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Cytisus infestus subsp. intermedius include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
